evidence_refs.quote: Large language models exhibit a "lost-in-the-middle" effect, retrieving information from the edges of a context more reliably than from its interior. We investigate whether a hard-to-easy reversal curriculum—training first on middle-position targets and expanding outward—can reduce this retrieval trough on a controlled synthetic key-value task.
